channel.title: Games on rirsc
channel.description: Recent content in Games on rirsc

item.title: 25week20 - Jabberwocky correlated
item.description:
The first stanza to what would become Jabberwocky↗: Stanza of Anglo-Saxon Poetry:

Twas bryllyg, and þe slythy toves
Did gyre and gymble in þe wabe:
All mimsy were þe borogoves;
And þe mome raths outgrabe.

˙
The World Record for the Shortest Math Article: 2 Words↗ And John Conway was involved, of course he was.

˙
Correlations are overused these days, primarily to reduce complex social problems into basic and extreme narratives. These are later transformed into political benefits for buffoons. Anyhow, this site is mostly for the lolz, and it does create some funny spurious correlations↗

˙
Place your cursor anywhere and wait↗

˙
Neal.fun↗ is probably one of the most famous people in creating these little games easy to share and equally fun and educational. In his internet artifacts, you can find an enormous amount of internet history facts, like the earliest lol↗

˙

Listening: The Tree of the Left Hand by Arianne Churchman & Benedict Drew

item.title: Lucas Pope is back
item.description:
Return of the Obra Dinn↗ is probably one of the best experiences I had ever have playing a videogame. The art style, the hard puzzles and all the secrets that the history hides. It was also nice cos I played this solo game with friends, so the experience was even cooler. Nothing better than struggling with other brains.

Anyway, I wrote this post to mention the new game from Lucas Pope↗ the creator of the Obra. Its called Mars After Midnight↗ I dont own a Playdate↗ so I wont be able to play it. But the art looks awesome and the approach sounds (too) similar to another of his gems; Papers Please↗
